# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

**WalkWars: 15-Minute Turf** — a turn-based strategy game played on real-world city maps. The player and an AI opponent compete to claim POIs (cafes, parks, attractions, etc.) generated by Nominatim. Each turn has a 15-minute walking budget (Action Points / AP). Real OSRM walking times — not straight-line distance — determine reachability, contests, and territory influence. The map is rendered with Folium / Leaflet.

Course: NTHU IE&IM, Spring 2026 final project. Solo (1 person).

## Companion Documents (READ THESE — IN THIS ORDER)

1. **`IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md`** — **read first.** Patch document with rule clarifications and bug fixes that override the spec where they disagree. The agent MUST treat these as canonical for the items listed there.
2. **`WalkWars_Project_Spec.md`** — the main specification. Defer to it except where `IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES.md` says otherwise.
3. **`WalkWars_Agent_Prompts.md`** — per-step task prompts. The student pastes one at a time. Do not jump ahead.
4. **`WalkWars_Proposal.pdf`** — submitted to TA. The build must match what the proposal promises.

## Student Context

The student is a **first-year IE/IM undergraduate at NTHU with minimal programming background**. They will be questioned on this code during a 7-minute final demo. A working repo that the student cannot explain is a failed deliverable. The student uses Claude Code with limited token budget (~2 hours of session time per ~5-hour cycle). **Be efficient with tokens. One step per session.**

## Teaching Mode (REQUIRED — DO NOT SKIP)

After EVERY task that creates or modifies code, produce these artifacts in order. This is non-negotiable; refer to spec Section 18 for exact format.

1. **Plain-English Walkthrough** for each file changed:
   - What this file does (1 paragraph, no jargon).
   - Key concepts introduced here.
   - Why we designed it this way.
   - When this runs in the game flow.

2. **Concept Cards** the first time any new concept appears in the project:
   Required cards include — Class, Dataclass, Decorator, Type hint, HTTP request / REST API, JSON, Flask route, Flask session, Jinja2 template, Cache, Heuristic algorithm, Bounding box, Geocoding, Pure function, GeoJSON, Greedy algorithm, Distance matrix, Weighted Voronoi diagram.
   Format: what it is (1 sentence), analogy, where it appears in our code, why we use it, likely professor question + good answer.

3. **3–5 Mock Q&A items** with model answers, covering at least: concept, walkthrough, design decision, modification scenario.

Do not skip Teaching Mode even if the student forgets to ask. If you must choose between writing more code and finishing Teaching Mode for code already written, **finish Teaching Mode first**.

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Language | Python 3.11+ |
| Web framework | Flask |
| Geocoding & POI search | Nominatim (OpenStreetMap) |
| Routing | OSRM public demo server (`router.project-osrm.org`) |
| Map rendering | Folium (Leaflet) |
| State persistence | Flask session (signed cookies) + disk JSON |
| Dependencies | Flask, folium, requests, python-dotenv |

**No additional packages without explicit student approval.** In particular: no Bootstrap, no React, no shapely, no networkx, no numpy.

## Commands

```bash
# Setup
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate           # Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Run development server
flask --app app run

# With debug mode
flask --app app run --debug

# Verify the app is up
curl http://localhost:5000/health
# Expected: {"status":"ok"}
```

## Planned Directory Structure

```
walkwars/
├── app.py                    # Flask app + routes (thin)
├── config.py                 # Constants, POI categories, game parameters
├── requirements.txt
├── README.md
├── .gitignore
├── .env.example
├── models/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── place.py              # Place dataclass (POI = game node)
│   └── game_state.py         # GameState, Player dataclasses
├── services/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── poi_service.py        # Nominatim POI generation
│   ├── osrm_service.py       # OSRM walking-time queries + matrix
│   ├── game_engine.py        # Turn loop, claim resolution, scoring
│   ├── ai_service.py         # Greedy AI opponent
│   └── map_service.py        # Folium map rendering
├── utils/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── coords.py             # Coordinate conversion helpers
│   └── decorators.py
├── templates/
│   ├── base.html
│   ├── index.html            # Setup screen (origin + start game)
│   └── game.html             # Game screen (map + turn controls)
├── static/
│   └── style.css
└── data/
    ├── cache/                # Each subdir has .gitkeep
    │   ├── poi/              # Nominatim POI search results
    │   └── osrm/             # OSRM walking-time queries
    └── games/                # Per-game state (matrix + history)
```

## Architecture

**Service-oriented.** All business logic lives in `services/`. `app.py` only wires routes to services. Keep `app.py` thin.

**Game state in Flask session + disk.** A small `GameState` (players, AP, owned POIs, turn number, game_id) is serialized into Flask's session cookie. The large data — the walking time matrix and POI list — is stored in `data/games/{game_id}.json`. This keeps cookies under 4KB while avoiding a database.

**Walking time matrix is the core data structure.** At game start, OSRM is queried pairwise for all POIs (cached on disk). Every gameplay decision — reachability, contest resolution, AI scoring, territory influence — reads from this precomputed matrix. **No OSRM calls happen mid-turn.**

**Greedy AI.** For each candidate move, compute `score = (value × combo_multiplier) / walking_time`. Pick the highest. Explainable, weak enough that the human can sometimes win, strong enough to be a credible opponent.

**Weighted Voronoi territory.** A neutral POI `x` is influenced by whichever player has the shortest `min over n in owned(p) of T[n][x]`. This is computed at the end of each turn and shown on the map by tinting neutral POIs with the influencing player's color (lighter shade).

**Why not full TSP / BFS / NetworkX:** This game does not need them. Reachability is `T[c][j] ≤ AP` (one lookup). Territory is `argmin` (one pass per neutral POI). AI is greedy (one pass per candidate). Keep the algorithm story small and explainable.

**Map injection:** Folium renders to an HTML string (`m.get_root().render()`); inject into Jinja2 template via `{{ map_html|safe }}`. Do not use Folium's `save()` to file.

## Critical Gotchas

**Coordinate order inconsistency** — different APIs use different conventions:
- Nominatim returns `(lat, lon)` (as strings; cast to float)
- OSRM URL path expects `lon,lat;lon,lat`
- OSRM `routes[0].geometry.coordinates` returns `[lon, lat]` pairs
- Folium expects `[lat, lon]`

**Always use helpers in `utils/coords.py`** — never inline coordinate-string building. This is the #1 source of bugs in this project.

**Nominatim ToS requirements**:
- Set a descriptive `User-Agent` header (`WalkWars/1.0 (NTHU final project)`)
- Rate-limit to 1 request/second (use `time.sleep(1.05)` between calls)
- Use `accept-language: zh-TW,en`

**OSRM is the hot path** — opening a game with 20 POIs requires `C(20,2) = 190` OSRM calls. Even with 0.05s latency that's ~10 seconds. **Mitigations:**
- All OSRM responses cached to disk by `(start, end)` key (rounded to 5 decimals).
- Matrix is computed once at game start, persisted, and reused for all turns.
- For the demo scenario, the cache must be pre-warmed (Step 12).
- During matrix build, log progress every 20 calls so the student knows it's not hung.

**Flask session size limit.** Signed cookies are capped around 4KB. Store **only**: `game_id`, current `turn`, current `current_player`. Load `GameState` (POIs, ownership map, matrix) from disk on each request via `game_id`.

**Game state mutation.** Flask sessions are dicts; modifying nested objects requires explicit `session.modified = True`. Always reassign the top-level key after mutation:
```python
gs = load_game_state(session['game_id'])
gs.turn += 1
save_game_state(session['game_id'], gs)
session['turn'] = gs.turn   # also update lightweight session copy
session.modified = True
```

**Contest resolution is deterministic.** When the AI moves to claim a POI the player already claimed this turn, compare OSRM walking times from each player's *pre-move* position. Shorter time wins. If equal, the player wins (favor human in tie-breakers for demo flow).

## Game Rules (Authoritative — see Spec Section 11 for full detail)

- **Setup:** Player enters origin (e.g., "清華大學"). System geocodes via Nominatim, generates 20–25 POIs in a 1.5 km radius.
- **Both players start at the origin.** No separate base selection in MVP.
- **Turns:** 6 total. Player moves first each turn, then AI.
- **AP per turn:** 15 walking-minutes. AP does not carry over (use it or lose it).
- **A move = walk to one POI within AP.** Stay-in-place is allowed (skip turn) but discouraged by scoring.
- **Claiming:** Walking to a POI claims it. Claimed POIs stay claimed unless contested (see below).
- **Contest:** If the AI targets a POI the player claimed this same turn, OSRM walking times from each player's pre-move position are compared. Shorter time wins. (In MVP, contests only happen on AI's turn against POIs the player claimed this same turn.)
- **Scoring at game end (turn 6):**
  - POI value sum (cafe=2, restaurant=2, park=3, station=3, attraction=5, shop=1, generic=1)
  - **Territory bonus:** +1 per neutral POI you influence (weighted Voronoi)
  - **Combo bonuses:**
    - "15-minute lifestyle circle": own ≥1 cafe + ≥1 park + ≥1 restaurant where pairwise walking time ≤ 15min → +8
    - "Tourist trail": own ≥3 attractions → +10
- **Winner:** highest total score.

## Build Order

Implement strictly in this sequence. Each step has a verification criterion that must pass before moving on (see `WalkWars_Agent_Prompts.md` for exact verification commands). **Do not skip ahead. Do not combine steps.**

### Phase 1 — Core MVP (must work for demo)

1. **Project scaffolding** — folder tree, config.py, requirements.txt, .gitignore, .env.example, minimal app.py with `/health` route.
2. **Place dataclass + POIService** — Nominatim search by category, returns ≥15 candidates for "清華大學".
3. **OSRMService + coords helpers** — single pairwise walking time query, cached.
4. **Walking time matrix** — given N POIs, build N×N matrix (with progress logging); cache to disk.
5. **GameState + Player models + new game route** — entering origin geocodes, generates POIs, builds matrix, redirects to `/game`.
6. **Game screen with Folium map** — markers for all POIs, origin highlighted, no interactivity yet.
7. **Player turn: select reachable POI + claim** — clicking a POI submits `/move`; reachable POIs highlighted.
8. **AI turn (Greedy)** — after player move, AI picks best POI; both moves applied.
9. **Contest resolution + running score panel** — same-turn conflicts resolved by walking time; score shown each turn.
10. **Game end + weighted Voronoi territory + combo bonuses** — turn 6 triggers end screen with final breakdown.

### Phase 2 — Polish (only after Phase 1 verifies end-to-end)

11. **UI styling** — color-coded markers (player blue, AI red, neutral gray), AP / turn / score panel, reachable POIs visually highlighted.
12. **Cache warming script** — `scripts/warm_cache.py` runs the demo scenario end-to-end to populate caches.
13. **README + demo dry-run** — write README; rehearse the 7-minute demo three times with a timer.

## When in Doubt

- **Adding a feature outside the spec**: stop and ask the student.
- **Editing files outside the current step's scope**: stop and ask.
- **Importing a package not in requirements.txt**: solve it with existing dependencies, or ask.
- **A "better" algorithm or design occurs to you**: do not implement. Mention it in Teaching Mode as a future-work note; defer to the spec's design.
- **You're not sure what the student wants**: ask one clarifying question rather than guessing.

## Demo Scenario (Locked)

- **Origin:** 清華大學 (NTHU) main gate, approx (24.7956, 120.9967)
- **POI radius:** 1500 m
- **POI count target:** 20–25
- **Turns:** 6
- **AP per turn:** 15 minutes
- **Opponent:** 1 Greedy AI

All caches must be pre-warmed for this scenario before demo day.
